morePage.title: Settings
morePage.fleetEmailHint: Document alerts and notifications will be sent to this address. It does not affect your login email.

driverProfile.notFoundDesc: Your account is not yet linked to a driver record. Please ask your manager to add you as a driver.
driverProfile.devLoginHint: Log in as any driver for testing
driverProfile.noDriversFound: No drivers found. Please add a driver first.
driverProfile.devTestHint: Log in as any user for testing

driverTruck.title: My Truck
driverTruck.noTruckDesc: You have not been assigned a truck. Please contact your manager.

fleetSetup.mustAcceptTerms: You must accept the Terms of Service and Privacy Policy to continue
fleetSetup.subtitle: Start by creating your fleet company
fleetSetup.tip: 💡 Tip: Once your fleet company is created, you can add trucks, drivers, and clients.

addDriver.title: Add Driver
addDriver.licenseExpiryHint: Leave blank and the driver shows up as "missing docs".
addDriver.tempPasswordHint: The driver will change their password on first sign-in. Leave blank to skip account creation.

dismissed.empty: You haven't dismissed any entries yet.
dismissed.emptyHint: Dismiss ignores specific rows from a single batch. If you want to skip a plate on every import, create a Subcontractor rule from the Unmatched plates tab — it applies going forward.

resolutions.title: Plate Rules
resolutions.description: These rules apply automatically on every new import — the Subcontractor button excludes plates from analytics, the Fix Plate button redirects misread plates to the correct ones. Deleting a rule only affects future imports; past entries remain unchanged.
resolutions.confirmDelete: Delete resolution for {{plate}}? Future imports will no longer apply this rule.

unmatched.title: All plates are matched.
unmatched.subtitle: Imported fuel entries are linked to your trucks.

banner.lead: These plates' records aren't matched to a truck and aren't in analytics. 4 ways to resolve:

createTruck.label: Create truck
createTruck.desc: Add the truck; past and new records link to it and join analytics.

alias.label: Fix plate
alias.desc: Redirect to the correct plate; records link to the right truck and join analytics.

subcontractor.label: Subcontractor
subcontractor.desc: This plate's records won't count in analytics. Cancel from 'Rules' anytime.

dismiss.label: Dismiss
dismiss.desc: Records from this import won't count in analytics. May reappear in another batch.

tooltips.createTruck: Register this plate as a new truck. Historical entries auto-link to the new truck.
tooltips.alias: Redirect a misread plate to the correct one (e.g. 34A8C123 → 34ABC123). This and future entries link to the correct truck.
tooltips.subcontractor: This plate belongs to a subcontractor. Excluded from fleet analytics; no truck is created.
tooltips.dismiss: Dismiss the {{count}} entries of this plate in "{{batch}}". The same plate may reappear in another batch.

aliasModal.title: Fix plate
aliasModal.description: Written as {{plate}} in the statement. Enter the correct plate; all records link to that truck and join analytics.

subcontractorModal.title: Mark as subcontractor
subcontractorModal.description: {{plate}} will be marked as subcontractor. This plate's past records won't count in analytics.
subcontractorModal.bullet1: Future imports are auto-marked subcontractor; won't appear in pending.
subcontractorModal.bullet2: You can delete this rule anytime from 'Rules'.

dismissModal.title: Dismiss in this batch
dismissModal.description: {{count}} records of {{plate}} from this import won't count in analytics.

confirmModal.title: Confirm duplicate
confirmModal.description: The flagged entry will be marked as a duplicate and deleted.
confirmModal.bullet1: The entry is marked deleted; excluded from queries and fleet analytics.
confirmModal.bullet2: Reversing this action isn't supported yet.

fuelReview.filteredByBatch: Showing only entries from the selected batch.

cookie.message: 🍪 Naklos uses only essential session cookies.
cookie.usingPlatform: By using the platform you accept our

efficiency.aggregateHint: Odometer data is sparse — estimated from total fuel and the earliest/latest readings.
efficiency.aggregateSource: Estimated due to sparse odometer data

delete.title: Delete fuel record
delete.description: Record from {{date}} ({{liters}} L) will be deleted.

error.photoBadType: Only JPG, PNG or WebP allowed.

fuelEntry.editPhotoHint: To change the photo, delete this entry and add a new one.

page.title: Import fuel file
page.mismatchTitle: This file doesn't match the chosen format
page.mismatchBody: We didn't recognize the column headers. No worries — let's get your file recognized in a second. One click creates a custom format, columns auto-mapped.
page.mismatchCta: Create a format for my file
page.possibleDupBody: These rows will be skipped by default. If you think a row is actually a different refuel, check the <1>Import anyway</1> box in that row.

error.dateUnrecognized: Couldn't read date: {{value}}. Accepted formats: DD.MM.YYYY, DD/MM/YYYY, YYYY-MM-DD (with optional time, e.g. 14.04.2026 08:35). Including the time helps us tell apart multiple fill-ups on the same day.
error.dateInFuture: Date can't be in the future: {{value}}.
error.numberNotPositive: {{field}} must be greater than zero. Got: {{value}}.

clone.title: Clone format
clone.description: Create a copy of this format and customize it for your fleet. The original stays untouched.

deactivate.title: Deactivate format
deactivate.description: "{{name}}" will be deactivated. It won't be selectable in the import screen anymore but stays linked to past batches.

allDuplicates.title: No new entries imported
allDuplicates.body: Every row in the uploaded file is already present.

mixed.title: {{imported}} imported, {{duplicates}} already exist
mixed.body: Some rows were skipped as duplicates.

truck.title: Archive truck
truck.description: Truck with plate {{plate}} will be archived.
truck.bulletHistory: Fuel and trip history are preserved
truck.bulletHidden: The truck no longer appears in lists
truck.bulletPlateReuse: The same plate can be reused for a new truck

driver.title: Delete driver
driver.bulletHistory: Past trip and fuel records are kept; driver field shows as "—"
driver.bulletTruck: If assigned to a truck, that truck's driver field clears

certificate.title: Delete {{type}}
certificate.description: {{type}} record will be deleted. Past document uploads stay.

unassignDriver.title: Unassign driver
unassignDriver.description: Driver assignment will be removed from this truck.

config.subtitle: These settings apply to the entire fleet.

settings.title: Fleet settings

enabled.label: Anomaly engine
enabled.hint: Disabling this stops all alerts from being detected.

digestHour.label: Daily digest hour
digestHour.hint: The daily email digest is sent at this hour (0–23).

instantCriticalEmail.label: Instant critical email
instantCriticalEmail.hint: Urgent alerts trigger an immediate email when detected.

readOnly.title: Read-only settings
readOnly.hint: Working hours and recipients are managed in a separate form.

settings.rescanNote: Existing entries will be re-scanned with the new settings.

tolerancePct.label: Tolerance
tolerancePct.hintTank: How far above tank capacity counts as an anomaly.
tolerancePct.hintOver: How much above the truck's baseline counts as an anomaly.
tolerancePct.hintUnder: How far below baseline is treated as possible bad data.
tolerancePct.hintPrice: Acceptable difference between computed total and receipt total.

minDeltaKm.label: Minimum distance
minDeltaKm.hint: Skip entries with less than this distance since the last fill — the ratio isn't reliable on short trips.

windowHours.label: Time window
windowHours.hint: A second refill within this window is flagged.

maxPerDay.label: Daily cap
maxPerDay.hint: More than this many refills per truck per day is flagged.

includeWeekends.label: Include weekends
includeWeekends.hint: Saturday–Sunday refills count as off-hours at any time.

minLitersThreshold.label: Minimum refill volume
minLitersThreshold.hint: Refills smaller than this (e.g. AdBlue) are skipped by this rule.

minEntries.label: Required entries
minEntries.hint: Trucks with fewer entries get a 'no baseline' notice.

rules.title: Alert rules
rules.subtitle: Toggle each rule and fine-tune its settings.
rules.rescanNote: Existing entries will be re-scanned with the new rules.

ODOMETER_ROLLBACK.desc: Fires when the odometer is lower than the previous entry.

VOLUME_EXCEEDS_TANK_CAPACITY.desc: Fires when the refueled volume exceeds the truck's tank capacity.

FUEL_TYPE_MISMATCH.desc: Fires when the receipt's fuel type differs from the truck's fuel type.

CONSUMPTION_OVER_BASELINE.desc: Fires when consumption exceeds the baseline by the configured ratio.

CONSUMPTION_UNDER_BASELINE.desc: Fires when consumption is far below baseline — likely data error.

RAPID_REFUEL.desc: Fires when the same truck refuels twice within a short window.

EXCESSIVE_DAILY_FREQUENCY.desc: Fires when a truck refuels unusually often within a single day.

OFF_HOURS_PURCHASE.desc: Refuels outside of configured working hours.

ODOMETER_NOT_ADVANCING.desc: Fires when consecutive refuels show no odometer change.

IMPLAUSIBLE_VOLUME_FOR_TYPE.desc: Volume looks implausible for this fuel type (e.g. diesel in an EV).

PRICE_MATH_MISMATCH.desc: Unit price × liters doesn't match the total amount on the receipt.

MISSING_BASELINE.desc: Baseline consumption hasn't been established from enough refuels yet.

overrides.subtitle: Baseline and rule configuration for this truck.
overrides.rescanNote: This truck's entries will be re-scanned with the new settings.
overrides.rulesEmpty: No custom rule overrides for this truck.

empty.title: All clear
empty.subtitle: No refuels need attention right now. New alerts will show up here.
empty.filteredSubtitle: Clear the filter to see all alerts.

EXCESSIVE_DAILY_FREQUENCY.title: Too many refuels in one day

modal.noComparison: No comparison data for this alert.

confirmHint.dataBroken: This faulty entry is already excluded from analysis — the wrong data isn't skewing the truck's fuel consumption average. Marking it as a real issue makes that exclusion permanent.
confirmHint.behaviour: This isn't a data error, it's a real event. Marking it doesn't change the truck's consumption average; it's just kept on record.

modal.fixDataHint: If the truck's setup is wrong (tank capacity, fuel type, baseline), fix it on the truck page — the next scan will auto-resolve this alert.

dismissBtn.title: Not an issue — dismiss
dismissBtn.hint: Dismiss if there's a reasonable explanation.

dismissReason.title: Why are you dismissing?

FALSE_POSITIVE.title: System detected it incorrectly
FALSE_POSITIVE.desc: The refuel was normal; the rule fired unnecessarily.

DATA_ENTRY_ERROR.title: Entry was wrong — I fixed it
DATA_ENTRY_ERROR.desc: Liters / odometer in the record were wrong; I updated them.

EXPLAINED_BY_DRIVER.title: Driver explained, it's normal
EXPLAINED_BY_DRIVER.desc: E.g. muddy road, heavy load, stop-and-go traffic.

dismissReason.notePlaceholder: What did the driver say, what did you do?
